questions.question: Calculate the work done when a force of 10 N displaces an object by 5 m in the direction of force.
questions.answer: Work done, W = F × d = 10 × 5 = 50 J.

questions.question: A body of mass 2 kg moves with velocity 3 m/s. Find its kinetic energy.
Find the potential energy of a 5 kg object at a height of 4 m. (Take g = 10 m/s²)
A force of 20 N acts at an angle of 60° to the displacement of 3 m. Calculate the work done.
If a body has KE = 50 J, what will be the work done to stop it?
questions.answer: Work done = change in kinetic energy = 50 J (negative, to stop the body).

questions.question: A 10 kg body is lifted to a height of 2 m. Calculate the work done against gravity. (g = 10 m/s²)
Find the power if 200 J of work is done in 10 s.
A car of mass 1000 kg is moving at 20 m/s. Find its kinetic energy.
A 50 N force is applied to push a box 5 m along the floor. Work done by friction is 20 J against motion. Net work done?
questions.answer: Work done by applied force = 50 × 5 = 250 J. Net work = 250 − 20 = 230 J.

questions.question: A body of mass 2 kg falls from a height of 5 m. Find its potential energy at the top and kinetic energy just before hitting the ground. (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.answer: PE = mgh = 2 × 10 × 5 = 100 J; KE just before hitting ground = 100 J (by conservation of energy).

questions.question: Work done by a body moving horizontally under a vertical force?
questions.answer: Zero, because the force is perpendicular to displacement.

questions.question: Find the work done when a force of 15 N moves an object 4 m at an angle of 0° to the displacement.
A 3 kg body is lifted to a height of 10 m. Calculate its potential energy. (g = 10 m/s²)
Find the velocity of a 2 kg body having 50 J of kinetic energy.
Work done by friction is considered positive or negative?
A 1000 J work is done in 50 s. Calculate the power.
A spring stores 25 J of energy when stretched. What type of energy is this?
If work done on a body is 100 J, what is the change in its kinetic energy?
questions.answer: Change in kinetic energy = 100 J (by work-energy theorem).

questions.question: A 5 kg body falls from 10 m height. Calculate speed just before hitting the ground. (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.answer: PE at top = mgh = 5×10×10 = 500 J = KE just before impact → 1/2 mv² = 500 → v² = 200 → v ≈ 14.14 m/s.

questions.question: State the work done by gravity on a freely falling body.
questions.answer: Positive, because gravity assists the motion of the body.

questions.question: A body is moving on a frictionless horizontal surface. Work done by friction?
questions.answer: Zero, since there is no friction.

questions.question: Define instantaneous velocity in terms of energy.
questions.answer: v = √(2 × KE / m), relates kinetic energy to instantaneous velocity.

questions.question: If a body moves under a force opposite to its motion, what type of work is done?
A body of mass 2 kg moves at 10 m/s. Find kinetic energy and power if it stops in 5 s.
questions.answer: KE = 1/2 × 2 × 10² = 100 J; Power = Work / Time = 100 / 5 = 20 W.

questions.question: A force of 10 N moves an object 5 m along the direction of the force. If the object moves in opposite direction, work done?
questions.answer: Work done = −50 J (negative, as force opposes displacement).
